items.q: How do you say 'I live in a house'?
items.why: Students drop the contraction and mismatch gender; 'maison' is feminine, so 'une maison'.
Students use the wrong preposition; means of transport usually take 'en' (en avion, en train).
Students guess wrongly; it is a budget place to stay, useful holiday/accommodation vocabulary.

items.q: How do you say 'I live in a house in the countryside'?
items.correct: J'habite dans une maison à la campagne.
items.random_wrong: J'habite un maison à la campagne.
items.why: It's J'habite dans une maison à la campagne.. “Je habite une maison campagne.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: en avion. “en train” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: 'Une auberge de jeunesse' is a type of:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Accommodation (youth hostel). “Restaurant” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Abroad. “At home” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
